LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT MIRABITO, MOONEY & ASSOCIATES' MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED IN PART; NO OPPOSITION FILED. THE COURT STRIKES THE FOLLOWING PARTS OF THE COMPLAINT; REQUEST FOR $15 MILLION IN DAMAGES, ALLEGATIONS OF FRAUD, NEGLIGENCE, BREACH OF RIGHT OF REDEMPTION AND "SPECIAL BUSINESS REGULATIONS," AND ALLEGATIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT ATTORNEY BRIAN AFFRUNTI. DEFENDANT TO FILE ANSWER WITHIN 10 DAYS OF THE FILING OF THE ORDER. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: JAMES WARREN, REPORTER: CHRISTINA PAXTON, CSR #1558
DEFENDANT(S) REJECTED PROPOSED ARBITRATOR PATRICK HART FABIAN.

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-09-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Michael K. Budra, Esq., Law Offices of Doris A. Squeri, 408-453-8878, for defendant and moving party Mirabito, Mooney & Associates
RULING:
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT MIRABITO, MOONEY & ASSOCIATES' MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED in part; no opposition filed. The Court strikes the following parts of the complaint; request for $15 million in damages, allegations of fraud, negligence, breach of right of redemption and "special business regulations," and allegations against Defendant attoney Brian Affrunti. Defendant to file answer within 10 days of the filing of the order. Order signed. (301/JLW/) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558
